title: Boondi Raita

ingredients.title: For the Raita Base
items: Sugar: ½ tsp (optional, balances the tanginess)

ingredients.title: For the Boondi
items: Water: ~¼ cup (to make a flowing batter)
Baking soda: a tiny pinch (optional, makes boondi light)
Oil or ghee: for deep frying

ingredients.title: For Tempering / Garnish
items: Fresh coriander leaves: 1 tbsp, finely chopped
Black salt (kala namak): ¼ tsp (optional, for authentic flavor)
Mint leaves: 5–6, finely chopped (optional, for freshness)

instruction.title: Step 1: Prepare Boondi
steps: Mix gram flour, salt, baking soda, and water to form a smooth, flowing batter (like cream consistency).
Heat oil in a deep pan over medium heat.
Hold a perforated spoon or boondi maker over the hot oil. Pour a little batter and tap gently so small droplets fall into the oil.
Fry until light golden and crisp. Remove and drain on paper towels.

instruction.title: Step 2: Prepare Yogurt Base
steps: Whisk yogurt until smooth and creamy. Add milk if you want extra richness.
Optional: lightly crush some of the boondi and mix into the yogurt so it soaks flavors without becoming soggy.

instruction.title: Step 3: Tempering (Optional but Recommended)
steps: Heat 1 tsp ghee in a small pan.
Add cumin seeds and let them crackle.
Add finely chopped green chili and mint leaves. Sauté briefly and pour over the prepared raita.

instruction.title: Step 4: Assemble
steps: Gently fold the crisp boondi into the yogurt base.
Sprinkle red chili powder, chaat masala, black salt, and fresh coriander on top.
Chill for 15–20 minutes before serving for best flavor.

chefTips: Use fresh, thick yogurt: Avoid watery curd; Greek yogurt works beautifully.
Fry boondi till just golden: Over-frying makes it hard; under-frying makes it soggy when mixed with yogurt.
Layer flavors: A light tempering of cumin and fresh herbs elevates the dish from simple to gourmet.
Serve cold, immediately: Boondi starts to soak yogurt quickly, so add it last if making ahead.
This raita pairs perfectly with Biryani, Pulao, or Tandoori dishes, giving a creamy, spiced contrast.
